author: Dr. Emily Brown
content:
Telephone encounter with patient's wife Patricia Morrison regarding lab results and medication refills.

REASON FOR CALL: Mrs. Morrison called requesting lab results from 8/14 draw and refills for several medications running low.

LAB RESULTS REVIEWED:
- BMP: Na 136, K 4.8 (slightly elevated but acceptable), Cl 101, HCO3 22, BUN 38, Cr 1.95 (stable at baseline), glucose 178
- CBC: WBC 7.2, Hgb 10.9 (mild anemia, stable), Hct 33.1, Plt 198
- A1c: 7.6% (improved from 7.8%)
- INR: 2.4 (therapeutic)
- BNP: 490 (mildly elevated above his baseline of ~450, will monitor)

Explained results to Mrs. Morrison. She was pleased that A1c improved. Discussed that potassium at 4.8 is acceptable but on the higher end given his Entresto and spironolactone — advised continued dietary potassium restriction and will recheck in 4 weeks. BNP mildly above recent baseline — asked about symptoms: Mrs. Morrison reports her husband has been doing well, no increased dyspnea, no worsening edema, weight stable at 97-98 kg. No acute changes warranting visit.

MEDICATION REFILLS:
- Gabapentin 300mg — sent to Walgreens on Main St. (90-day supply)
- Metformin 250mg — sent to Walgreens (90-day supply)
- Tamsulosin 0.4mg — sent to Walgreens (90-day supply)
- Warfarin 5mg — sent to Walgreens (30-day supply per anticoagulation clinic protocol)
- Vitamin D3 2000 IU — OTC, reminded patient can purchase at pharmacy

Verified all other medications have adequate supply per Mrs. Morrison. She confirms he is taking everything as prescribed.

ADDITIONAL CONCERNS: Mrs. Morrison asked about the colonoscopy that was discussed — it has been scheduled for September 8th. She received the prep instructions and warfarin bridging protocol from GI office. She wants to confirm the enoxaparin dosing — directed her to call GI office for the specific bridging protocol letter, which includes dates for stopping warfarin and starting/stopping enoxaparin. Reminded her that if any questions about the bridge, Dr. Wilson's cardiology office can also assist.

She also mentioned that her husband has been having more knee pain and asked about whether he can take Advil (ibuprofen). STRONGLY advised against ibuprofen — patient is on warfarin (bleeding risk), has CKD Stage 3b (nephrotoxicity), and has HFrEF (volume retention). Reinforced that acetaminophen 500mg up to 2g/day and topical diclofenac gel are his approved options. If pain worsening, recommended PCP or orthopedic evaluation.

PLAN:
1. All refills sent to pharmacy as above.
2. Recheck BMP in 4 weeks — K monitoring.
3. No change to current management.
4. Colonoscopy 9/8 with warfarin bridging — coordinate with GI and cardiology.
5. NSAID avoidance reinforced — documented in chart.
6. Follow-up labs as above. Next PCP visit already scheduled.

Total call duration: 22 minutes.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Emily Brown
Date: 8/19/2023
